lifecycle.note: A source-backed research artifact with explicit confidence.

blocks.title: A source-backed brief an agent can update safely
blocks.lede: Use Dossier for research synthesis, competitive scans, vendor comparisons, or decision support. The important part is not just prose, it is claims tied to sources, assumptions, and next verification steps.

cards.title: What changed
cards.body: Teams want docs that double as stateful workflows for agents.

cards.title: What to verify
cards.body: Pricing, licensing, and current hosted editor integrations should be checked against official sources.

cards.title: What to decide
cards.body: Pick whether this artifact is a handoff, a decision record, or a living operating surface.

items.statement: Research briefs are more reusable when claims are structured separately from prose.
Pricing and vendor claims require a fresh source check before publication.

sources.label: Dossier repository
sources.summary: Source, examples, schemas, and package metadata.

sources.label: Example gallery
sources.summary: Local examples demonstrate multiple use cases.

claims.claim: A dossier can package prose, tables, charts, claims, and packet state in one file.
Public market or pricing statements should be rechecked before publication.

steps.title: Gather
steps.body: Collect official docs, changelogs, repos, and source URLs.

steps.title: Separate
steps.body: Keep observations, assumptions, and claims in different blocks.

steps.title: Decide
steps.body: Use the matrix and risk register to make tradeoffs explicit.

steps.title: Refresh
steps.body: Agents update only stale claims and preserve prior evidence.

risks.mitigation: Use official sources and record retrieval dates.
Use trust-report status and confidence per claim.

items.label: Repository
items.use: Confirm schema, examples, and release notes.
